name: JEDI-3hyp
description: JEDI-2P variant with two-photon voltage sensitivity shifted toward hyperpolarized potentials for detecting subthreshold fluctuations below resting membrane potential.

spectrum.name: JEDI-3hyp
spectrum.note: No 1P excitation/emission spectrum is published for JEDI-3hyp; the JEDI3 paper (doi:10.1038/s41592-026-03043-8, Fig. 1c) reports only a 2P excitation spectrum (peak 940 nm) and states the JEDI3 indicators have excitation profiles similar to the parent JEDI-2P. The JEDI3 mutations (Fig. 1a) lie in the voltage-sensing domain, not the cpGFP chromophore environment, and do not revert the T207H (GFP T203H) red-shift that JEDI-2P carries, so the 1P spectrum is effectively identical to JEDI-2P. The displayed 1P custom curve is therefore inherited verbatim from the direct parent JEDI-2P (real 1P spectrum digitized from Cell 2022, Fig. S4J: excitation peak ~501 nm, emission peak ~519 nm). Previously this page carried the older ASAP4/cpGFP curve (489/511 nm), which was byte-identical to a superseded, mis-attributed JEDI-2P copy and ~10-12 nm too blue.

kinetics.note: On (−70→+30 mV activation) is the amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit in Table 1: τfast 0.48 ms (90.3%) + τslow 7.97 ms (9.7%) = 1.21 ms (the stored value was previously only the fast component, 0.48 ms). Off (+30→−70 mV deactivation) is the monoexponential τ of 2.11 ms. Measured under widefield 1P illumination in voltage-clamped HEK293A cells at 33°C.

dynamicRangeData.note: Response to a 100-mV depolarizing step (-70 mV to +30 mV) at 23°C in HEK293A cells under 2P excitation at 940 nm. The 51.5% value is stated in the main text (compared with 63.5% for JEDI3sub and 41.8% for JEDI-2P) and reported in Supplementary Fig. 3/Supplementary Table 2; the F-V curve is shown in Fig. 1h. F-V shifted toward hyperpolarized potentials: -%ΔF/Fo reaches ~+91% at -120 mV (Fig. 1h).

Response amplitude to a 100-mV voltage step (−70 mV to +30 mV) at 33°C in HEK293A cells under 2P resonant scanning at 940 nm (Extended Data Fig. 1b, value 51.72%). This is the 33°C counterpart of the 23°C Fig. 1h value (-51.5%). Compare ASAP5 35.20%, JEDI-2P 41.73%, JEDI3sub 60.26% in the same panel.

sensitivityData.note: Peak -%ΔF/Fo response to a 2-ms FWHM single-spike waveform at 23°C under 2P excitation at 940 nm in HEK293A cells (Fig. 1j, n = 7). Compare JEDI-2P 23.3% and JEDI3sub 42.8% in the same experiment.

apWidthData.sample: HEK293A cells, whole-cell voltage clamp, in vitro (2-ms FWHM command AP waveform)
apWidthData.note: Optical FWHM (width of the fluorescence response to a single-spike waveform) of JEDI3hyp, printed value from Fig. 1k. Driven by a 2-ms FWHM command AP waveform under 2P excitation at 940 nm, 23°C, HEK293A cells (n = 7). Data collected at 80 kHz, downsampled to 20 kHz; FWHM taken after 20-kHz linear interpolation. Statistically indistinguishable from JEDI3sub (6.6 ms); slightly wider than JEDI-2P (5.6 ms, *). The much larger value vs the in-vivo Fig. 2d measurement (~1.2 ms) reflects the slow 23°C kinetics plus the broad 2-ms command waveform.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse visual cortex, awake behaving, in vivo, soma-targeted JEDI3hyp-Kv (ULoVE 2P)
apWidthData.note: Optical spike-width FWHM of JEDI3hyp-Kv from real cortical action potentials recorded in vivo with ULoVE 2P microscopy (920 nm, ~7 kHz frame rate) in awake mice (n = 12 cells). Digitized from the unlabeled Fig. 2d spike-width bar chart (no printed value; +/-~0.1 ms); the paper states spike widths were statistically indistinguishable across indicators (Supplementary Table 2; JEDI-2P-Kv and JEDI3sub-Kv similar). The much smaller value vs the in-vitro Fig. 1k measurement (6.3 ms) reflects the faster kinetics at 37 C plus the real (sharper) cortical spike vs the broad 2-ms command waveform used in vitro.

photostabilityData.note: ~52% fluorescence remaining after 6 min under 2P resonant scanning (940 nm, 37 mW at the sample plane, 440 Hz) at 23°C in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells (Fig. 1e, n = 4). Power reported as 37 mW at the sample plane (not an irradiance), so not directly comparable to the 100 mW/mm² 1P standard. Comparable to JEDI-2P (~48-50%) and JEDI3sub (~50%) in the same experiment (Fig. 1f, similar across 29-100 mW range).

photobleach.note: Model-free photostability: t75% = time for fluorescence to fall to 75% of initial. Digitized from the JEDI3hyp curve in the representative 6-min 2P photobleaching trace (Fig. 1e, 37-mW excitation at the sample plane, HEK293A cells, n=3-4). Bi-exponential fit (fast tau1, slow tau2). This is a 2P, power-only measurement (not mW/mm2), so it stands alone and is NOT intensity-normalized or comparable to 1P GEVIs.

researchPapers.title: Designer indicators for two-photon recording of subthreshold voltage dynamics
researchPapers.authors: Land MA, Galdamez M, Villette V, Zhu J, Lu X, Marosi M, et al.
researchPapers.sample: HEK293 cells, in vitro; Rat, cultured hippocampal neurons, in vitro; Mouse, visual cortex L2/3, in vivo; Mouse, hippocampus CA1 PV+ interneurons, in vivo; Mouse, cortex VIP+/SOM+ interneurons, in vivo
